subject: Geography
title: Thwaites: The Doomsday Glacier
description: A micro-lesson that makes Thwaites: The Doomsday Glacier usable.

hook.text: Thwaites Glacier in Antarctica is nicknamed the 'Doomsday Glacier' for good reason.

buildup.text: It's the size of Florida and holds enough ice to raise global sea levels by 65 cm.

discovery.text: Warm water is melting it from below, and its grounding line is retreating rapidly.

twist.text: If Thwaites collapses, it could destabilize neighboring glaciers—triggering 3 meters of sea rise.

climax.text: Scientists call it the most important glacier on Earth for predicting our coastal future.

punchline.text: One glacier holds the fate of every coastal city on the planet.

quiz.question: Why is Thwaites Glacier called the 'Doomsday Glacier'?
options: Its collapse could trigger massive sea level rise
